victim.value: Sabrina, haunted by a ghost named Jessica who physically harms her and kills her dog
victim.quote: she took a knife and carved a J into my arm. It was horribly painful

culprit_and_motive.value: Jessica the ghost, motive unclear - possibly seeking attention or connection
culprit_and_motive.quote: When she cry's, it is blood. Her tears are made of blood.

investigator.value: Sabrina herself, the victim trying to understand why she's being haunted
investigator.quote: I still haven't figured out why I see her or why she haunts me.

red_herring.value: Mother's disbelief and assumption that self-harm explains the supernatural evidence
red_herring.quote: Since I used to be a cutter, she thinks I carved the J into my skin.

clue_fairness.value: Physical evidence presented to reader: blood on floor, carved J, dog's death, broken window
clue_fairness.quote: she asked me where all the blood came from

social_world.value: Mother-daughter power dynamic where adult authority dismisses child's experience
social_world.quote: my mother doesn't even believe me. She thinks that I'm faking everything.

media_and_audience.value: Medical/psychiatric framework used to explain supernatural events as mental illness
media_and_audience.quote: she took me to the doctor for anti-depressant

investigator_wound.value: Narrator's history of self-harm and suicidal ideation makes her unreliable witness to her own experience
investigator_wound.quote: Since I used to be a cutter, she thinks I carved the J into my skin.
